primary_concern: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI) Risk with Concurrent Anemia
clinical_summary: Elevated serum creatinine (2.6 mg/dL) meets KDIGO criteria for AKI, indicating acute renal dysfunction. Concurrent hemoglobin of 9.7 g/dL suggests moderate anemia. No symptomatic findings reported at this time. Sepsis-3 criteria not yet met despite creatinine elevation, requiring further monitoring for infection or organ dysfunction.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine elevation exceeds 1.5× baseline threshold per KDIGO definition
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5× baseline in 7 days

prioritized_risks.explanation: Single organ dysfunction (kidney) identified; requires additional criteria for sepsis diagnosis
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2 points

recommended_actions: Assess for acute kidney injury etiology (pre-renal, intrinsic, post-renal)
Evaluate urine output and volume status per KDIGO management guidelines
Review baseline creatinine to confirm 1.5× increase threshold
Consider blood transfusion if hemoglobin < 7 g/dL or active bleeding
Monitor for signs of sepsis (fever, leukocytosis, hypotension)
doctor_handoff: Review baseline renal function, assess for infection sources, and confirm urine output documentation. Consider cross-matching for potential transfusion if anemia worsens.

disease_progression.observation: Creatinine elevation to 2.6 mg/dL with concurrent hemoglobin of 9.7 g/dL
